auth_prerequisites: Provider-backed evaluation requires configuring a supported model provider after install.

non_interactive_execution.summary: Public CLI docs and the published package show a deterministic installable terminal entrypoint.
non_interactive_execution.notes: Documented CLI install surface is public and stable enough for wrapper invocation.

offline_strategy.summary: A wrapper can validate parser behavior with captured fixtures or a fake opencode binary before provider-backed runs.
offline_strategy.notes: The recommendation lane only needs fixture and fake-binary coverage, not live provider execution.

observable_cli_surface.summary: The repo and CLI docs expose a terminal-facing command surface that is externally observable.
observable_cli_surface.notes: Observable CLI surface is grounded in public docs and repo presentation.

redaction_fit.summary: Text-stream terminal IO can pass through the repo redaction boundary before persistence.
redaction_fit.notes: Repo-fit inference is based on the agent shipping as an external terminal program.

crate_first_fit.summary: Documented CLI install channels support a wrapper-crate-first integration before any agent_api adapter work.
crate_first_fit.notes: Installable external CLI posture matches the repo crate-first onboarding ladder.

reproducibility.summary: Public docs and npm distribution allow repeatable local setup, with provider auth deferred as a later step.
reproducibility.notes: Local reproducibility is public; provider-backed evaluation remains a later gated step.

future_leverage.summary: Active upstream velocity and broad adoption make opencode strategically relevant even though it is already onboarded here.
future_leverage.notes: Current adoption and release activity make it a credible strategic contender.

blocked_steps: Provider-backed evaluation still needs maintainer credentials after local CLI install.
normalized_caveats: Use public install/docs evidence for recommendation; defer paid-provider behavior to onboarding proving.
